steps.title: Identify the structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: Left side: an incomplete independent clause ending with “...were ancient Egyptian units of”. Right side: a phrase about what the units were used for (“to record length and volume, respectively”) followed by another complete independent clause (“the djeser and heqat have since fallen out of use”).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
measurement. Used places a period after “measurement,” properly separating the two independent clauses into distinct sentences. The second sentence opens with “Used to record length and volume, respectively,” a participial phrase that modifies the subject “the djeser and heqat.”

Two other options both create comma splices by using only a comma between the independent clauses, and D forms an unparallel compound pairing “measurement” (noun) with “used” (past participle).

The period correctly separates the two independent clauses according to standard punctuation rules. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
